description_long: This is a large, rectangular hostel building complex with a central open courtyard, surrounded by three main wings and a smaller detached structure. The roofs are pitched and covered in a reddish-brown material, likely terracotta tiles, giving the building a Mediterranean or Colonial appearance. The exterior walls are predominantly white or light gray, with vertical window slits and regularly spaced columns or pilasters along the facades. The main entrance is accessible via a paved driveway that leads directly to the central wing, and the building features multiple levels with rows of windows indicating dormitory rooms. The detached structure appears to be a smaller annex or utility building, matching the main complex in style and materials. The overall design is functional yet visually cohesive, suitable for accommodating a large number of residents in an urban neighborhood.
description: A large, rectangular hostel complex with a central courtyard, red-tiled pitched roofs, and white facades featuring rows of windows and columns. Includes a main building and a smaller detached annex.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, central courtyard, and the driveway leading up to the building.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the main wings, the symmetry of the window arrangement, and the detached annex to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full rectangular layout, central courtyard, and the annex separated from the main structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the annex and the extended driveway, as well as the rear facade of the main building.
attribute_entrance_design: The main entrance is centrally located on the front facade, accessible via a wide paved driveway. It features a recessed entryway with a flat overhang, flanked by columns or pilasters. The entrance is large and rectangular, likely with double doors, and is visually emphasized by the symmetry of the surrounding architecture.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in regular, evenly spaced rows along all facades, with vertical rectangular shapes. The windows are narrow and tall, set between columns or pilasters, creating a rhythmic pattern. There are no visible decorative frames or shutters.
attribute_architectural_style: Colonial with Mediterranean influences, characterized by red-tiled pitched roofs, white facades, and symmetrical window arrangements.
